segments.text: "Welcome to Daily Robotics Digest, I'm your host.
It's July 19th, 2026 - a new day in the world of robotics.
From AI advancements to breakthroughs in automation, we've got you covered with our top stories today.
Join us as we dive into coherence in human-robot interaction and more."
From The Robot Report, "Palm Garden AI develops Coherence Guard relational decision layer for human-facing robots"
, Palm Garden AI has unveiled its new Coherence Guard relational decision layer designed to enable service robots to behave appropriately around people.

This system incorporates signals such as timing, proximity, emotional tone, trust preservation, respectful withdrawal, and socially appropriate action before or during execution, aiming to evaluate proposed actions for relational coherence in real-world human environments.
Coherence Guard's software is built on a flexible architecture that can run on edge devices, premises, or clouds, complementing existing safety systems rather than replacing them.
The patent-pending system will be available for licensed use through a SaaS model, with optional components for configuration, simulation support, and updates.
By integrating these elements, Palm Garden AI's Coherence Guard is poised to enhance the performance of human-facing robots in various settings.
The development of Coherence Guard marks an important step forward for the AI industry, as it addresses the need for more sophisticated social intelligence in service robots.
As the technology continues to evolve, users can expect to see improved behavior from these robots in real-world environments, with a focus on ensuring safe and respectful interactions with humans.
"And that's a wrap for today's robotic rundown!

I hope you're recharged and ready to tackle the day with robots that are a little more coherent, thanks to the innovative Coherence Guard from Palm Garden AI - talk about a 'growing' concern for human-robot relations!
